dialogue_events.dialogue_text: Lay your hands upon the shoulder of John Carter,
dialogue_events.source_excerpt: "Lay your hands upon the shoulder of John Carter," she said to them, turning toward me, "the man to whom Helium owes her princess as well as her victory today."

speaker.notes: Attributed through a role label rather than a canonical name.

dialogue_events.dialogue_text: the man to whom Helium owes her princess as well as her victory today.
dialogue_events.source_excerpt: "Lay your hands upon the shoulder of John Carter," she said to them, turning toward me, "the man to whom Helium owes her princess as well as her victory today."

speaker.notes: First-person narration suggests John Carter as the speaker.

dialogue_events.dialogue_text: You owe your thanks more to another man than to me,
dialogue_events.source_excerpt: "You owe your thanks more to another man than to me," I said, "and here he is; meet one of Barsoom's greatest soldiers and statesmen, Tars Tarkas, Jeddak of Thark."

speaker.notes: First-person narration suggests John Carter as the speaker.

dialogue_events.dialogue_text: and here he is; meet one of Barsoom's greatest soldiers and statesmen, Tars Tarkas, Jeddak of Thark.
dialogue_events.source_excerpt: "You owe your thanks more to another man than to me," I said, "and here he is; meet one of Barsoom's greatest soldiers and statesmen, Tars Tarkas, Jeddak of Thark."

speaker.notes: First-person narration suggests John Carter as the speaker.

dialogue_events.source_excerpt: "That Tardos Mors," he said, earnestly, "may meet the greatest living warrior of Barsoom is a priceless honor, but that he may lay his hand on the shoulder of a friend and ally is a far greater boon."

speaker.notes: No canonical speaker could be inferred from the source context.

dialogue_events.source_excerpt: "Welcome, my son," he said; "that you are granted, gladly, and without one word of opposition, the most precious jewel in all Helium, yes, on all Barsoom, is sufficient earnest of my esteem."
